sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Snowflake connector syncs database metadata — databases, schemas, and tables — from your Snowflake account into the Shielva knowledge base using the Snowflake SQL API v2. It authenticates via username + password (POST /session/v1/login-request), obtains a session token cached with a 1-hour TTL, and automatically re-authenticates when the token expires. The connector implements exponential backoff retry (skipping auth errors), per-status-code exception mapping, and three-phase sync (databases → schemas → tables).

subsections.title: Username + Password (Session Token)
subsections.content: The connector POSTs to POST /session/v1/login-request with the configured username and password. Snowflake returns a session token valid for up to 4 hours; the connector conservatively refreshes it after 1 hour. The token is sent as Authorization: Snowflake Token="{token}" on all subsequent API calls.
steps: In Snowflake: create a dedicated service account user (e.g. SHIELVA_USER).
Create a role (e.g. SHIELVA_CONNECTOR_ROLE) and grant USAGE on the databases and schemas you want to sync.
Grant USAGE on a virtual warehouse for query execution.
Enter the account identifier (without .snowflakecomputing.com), username, and password in the Shielva connector config.
Optionally specify a warehouse, default database, and role to activate.

fields.label: Account Identifier
fields.description: Your Snowflake account identifier. Found in your login URL: https://<account>.snowflakecomputing.com. Do not include the .snowflakecomputing.com suffix.

fields.label: Username
fields.description: Snowflake username for the service account.

fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the Snowflake service account. Stored encrypted at rest.

fields.label: Warehouse (optional)
fields.description: Virtual warehouse to use for query execution. Uses the user's default warehouse if not set.

fields.label: Default Database (optional)
fields.description: Default database context for SQL execution. All accessible databases are still discovered during sync.

fields.label: Role (optional)
fields.description: Snowflake role to activate for the session. Uses the user's default role if not set.

sections.title: Sync Engine
sections.content: The sync() method performs a three-phase sync: Phase 1 lists all accessible databases and normalizes them. Phase 2 lists schemas for each database. Phase 3 lists tables for each schema. Failures in schema or table phases are skipped (not fatal) — the connector returns COMPLETED (zero failures), PARTIAL (some failures), or FAILED (fatal database-list error). Each record is normalized into a ConnectorDocument with a stable SHA-256[:16] source_id.

methods.description: Validates credentials by calling POST /session/v1/login-request. Returns HEALTHY + CONNECTED on success, OFFLINE + MISSING_CREDENTIALS when required fields are absent, OFFLINE + INVALID_CREDENTIALS on auth failure.
Authenticates and executes SELECT CURRENT_USER(), CURRENT_ACCOUNT(), CURRENT_WAREHOUSE(). Returns HEALTHY (with username + account), DEGRADED (transient network error), or OFFLINE (auth failure).
Three-phase sync of databases, schemas, and tables. Returns documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed, and SyncStatus.
GET /api/v2/databases — list all accessible Snowflake databases.
GET /api/v2/databases/{database}/schemas — list schemas in a database.

methods.signature: async def list_tables(database: str, schema: str) -> list[dict]
methods.description: GET /api/v2/databases/{database}/schemas/{schema}/tables — list tables in a schema.

methods.signature: async def execute_query(sql: str, warehouse: str | None = None, ...) -> dict
methods.description: POST /api/v2/statements — execute a SQL statement. Returns inline result or a statementHandle for async polling.
GET /api/v2/statements/{handle} — poll for an async statement result.

sections.title: Data Model
sections.description: Every Snowflake entity is normalized into a ConnectorDocument. The source_id is a stable 16-character hex prefix of SHA-256(entity_type + ':' + qualified_name).

fields.name: content
fields.description: Newline-delimited key-value representation of the entity's properties.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: Empty string — Snowflake web console URLs are account-specific and not universally resolvable.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Entity-type-specific properties. Always includes entity_type key.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeError
exceptions.description: Base exception. Carries message, status_code, and code.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeAuthError
exceptions.description: Raised on 401/403 or when Snowflake returns success=false on login. Not retried.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeNetworkError
exceptions.description: Raised on httpx.TimeoutException or httpx.NetworkError. Retried with exponential backoff.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeNotFoundError
exceptions.description: Raised on 404. Carries resource name and resource_id.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeRateLimitError
exceptions.description: Raised on 429. Carries retry_after (seconds). Retried after the specified delay.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeQueryError
exceptions.description: Raised when Snowflake returns a SQL execution error. Carries sql_state and query_id.

exceptions.name: SnowflakeServerError
exceptions.description: Raised on 5xx responses from Snowflake. Retried.

items.cause: Wrong username or password, or the user account is locked.
items.solution: Verify credentials. Reset password with ALTER USER <username> RESET PASSWORD in Snowflake.

items.cause: Snowflake Network Policy blocks the Shielva egress IP.
items.solution: Add the Shielva egress IP range to the Snowflake Network Policy for this user.

items.cause: The service account role lacks USAGE privilege on the database.
items.solution: GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE <db> TO ROLE SHIELVA_CONNECTOR_ROLE.

items.cause: Too many concurrent SQL API requests.
items.solution: The connector retries automatically. Consider reducing sync frequency for very large accounts.

items.cause: Transient network error or Snowflake service disruption.
items.solution: Retry the health check. Check status.snowflake.com for outages.

items.cause: One or more API records returned unexpected data causing normalization to fail.
items.solution: Check connector logs. The connector skips inaccessible databases/schemas — grant appropriate privileges.
